articles.title: Adapt or die: the Australian live music lovers trying to reinvent an ‘ancient’ and ‘unviable’ industry
articles.description: With punters drinking less, running costs rising and one-third of the sector wiped out by Covid, venues and promoters are turning the business model on its head

articles.title: Canada to buy 12 hi-tech German submarines after bidding war
articles.description: TKMS beats South Korean rival to multibillion-dollar contract that will deepen Canada’s Nato ties

articles.title: ‘Charged with horror and anguish’: Arthur Boyd’s nightmarish Nebuchadnezzar series opens in major new Bundanon show
articles.description: Not for the faint-hearted, this is the first large-scale presentation of the Australian artist’s anti-Vietnam war, hallucinatory works since the late 60s

more.title: Expert links SA's economic struggles to State capacity, not immigration
more.description: Georgina Campbell from the Wild Law Institute said citizens' economic frustration is often misdirected at immigrants.

more.title: Transport Minister Barbara Creecy applauds early success of logistics sector reforms
more.description: Addressing the Southern African Transport Conference in Johannesburg, Creecy outlined progress in rail and port reforms, further stressing the need for a more sustainable and resilient transport system.

more.title: South Africa to Host Landmark 2026 SADC Industrialisation Week in Durban
more.description: DURBAN, KwaZulu-Natal — South Africa is preparing to welcome regional and international delegates to the Durban International Convention Centre later this month for the 2026 Annual SADC Industrialisation Week (SIW), a flagship event positioned as the largest public-private platform driving industrial development across the Southern African Development Community. Scheduled for July 27–31, 2026, the week-long [...]

more.title: Brazen passenger arrested with 50kg of khat at Cape Town airport
more.description: A 46-year-old man has been arrested after police discovered more than 50kg of khat concealed in his luggage at Cape Town International Airport.

more.title: South Africans crowned the world’s most wildlife obsessed travellers
more.description: For many South Africans, planning a holiday isn’t just about beaches, luxury hotels or famous landmarks—it’s about the chance to see incredible wildlife up close, Cape {town} Etc reports. A new international survey has found that South Africans are more passionate about wildlife-focused travel than people anywhere else in the world, highlighting just how deeply nature is woven into the country’s... Read more → The post South Africans crowned the world’s most wildlife obsessed travellers appeared first on Cape Town ETC .

more.title: Regis Resources meets top end of full-year gold guidance
more.description: ASX-listed Regis Resources has delivered a 12% quarter-on-quarter increase in gold production for the three months ended June 30. The quarter's output of 101 500 oz brings the group's total 2026 financial year production to 379 000 oz - just shy of the company's top-end of guidance of 380 000 oz.

more.title: Financial services firm banks on Google Cloud, ChromeOS
more.description: Radical Cloud Solutions has moved financial services firm Wealthtec to Google in a landmark roll-out.

more.title: Springboks still No 1 but All Blacks are closing in
more.description: The Springboks remain top of the World Rugby Men's Rankings after beating England, but New Zealand have narrowed the gap following their victory over France.

more.title: Lt-Gen Nhlanhla Mkhwanazi’s July 6 media briefing ― a year on
more.description: Today is exactly a year since KwaZulu-Natal police commissioner Lt-Gen Nhlanhla Mkhwanazi dropped the bombshell about criminality infiltrating the police and judicial system.

more.description: South Africa must confront the structural causes of xenophobia while firmly refusing to allow these grievances to manifest as violence against fellow Africans, writes John Akinribido

more.title: AARTO Phase 2 Rollout Begins Across 62 South African Municipalities
more.description: PRETORIA, Gauteng — The highly anticipated AARTO Phase 2 rollout has officially commenced, marking a significant shift in how South Africa handles road traffic offenses. Following years of legal battles, bureaucratic delays, and intense municipal resistance, the Administrative Adjudication of Road Traffic Offences (AARTO) system is now binding 62 municipalities across the country as of [...]

more.title: IEC targets AI bots and deepfakes in new campaign rules
more.description: Draft rules would require political parties to monitor online platforms, label AI-generated content and report suspected disinformation
